label: section3_q3_fea_mesh

messages_in.content: What FEA mesh refinement standard does Ridgeline use, and is that consistent with industry best practice?

args.question: What FEA mesh refinement standard does Ridgeline use?

chunks.text:
RIDGELINE ENGINEERING PARTNERS — FEA BEST PRACTICES GUIDE
Document Number: ENG-FEA-BP-01, Revision 4
Effective Date: April 2024
Author: Dr. Sarah Hendricks, PE, PhD
Approved by: Brian Tanaka, PE, Director of Engineering

This guide establishes minimum requirements for finite element analysis performed at Ridgeline. All FEA work must comply with these practices regardless of software platform (ANSYS, Abaqus, NASTRAN, CSiBridge, COMSOL, etc.).

1. MODEL PLANNING
Before constructing any FEA model,

chunks.text:
or code requirements that will be checked.

2. MESH QUALITY AND CONVERGENCE
All FEA models must include a mesh convergence study with a minimum of three refinement levels. The analyst must demonstrate that the key result (stress, displacement, temperature, etc.) changes by less than 5% between the second and third refinement levels. Results from the converged mesh are reported. If convergence is not achieved in three levels, additional refinement is required. The mesh convergence study must be
chunks.title: FEA Best Practices Guide

chunks.text:
RIDGELINE ENGINEERING PARTNERS — TECHNICAL STANDARDS GUIDE
Revision 12, Updated September 2024
Maintained by: Lisa Fernandez, PE, Director of Quality

This document lists the primary technical standards referenced across Ridgeline's practice areas. Engineers must use the editions listed below unless a client contract specifies otherwise. Copies of all listed standards are available in the firm's digital standards library (SharePoint > Technical Resources > Standards Library).

STRUCTURAL ANALYSI
chunks.title: Technical Standards Reference Guide

result.summary: 3 chunks retrieved. Top match: DOC-017 — FEA Best Practices Guide (score 0.6853).

final_message:
Ridgeline Engineering Partners' FEA mesh refinement standard, per their FEA Best Practices Guide (ENG-FEA-BP-01, Revision 4), requires:

- A mesh convergence study with at least three refinement levels.
- The analyst must demonstrate that the key result (such as stress, displacement, or temperature) changes by less than 5% between the second and third refinement levels.
- Results from the converged mesh are reported; if convergence is not achieved within three levels, further refinement is required.

Is this consistent with industry best practice?
Yes, this approach aligns closely with industry standards for FEA, which commonly require mesh convergence studies and set thresholds (typically between 3–5%) for result changes to verify solution stability. This ensures accuracy while balancing computational efficiency and is considered an industry best practice.

Let me know if you need direct references to industry standards or more details from Ridgeline's internal procedures.
